+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8d80
+project: local-review
+title: Operation-based `review` MCP tool (thin wrapper over the engine)
+---
+## What
+Expose the engine as an OPERATION-BASED MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/`, dispatched by an `op` field exactly like the existing `git`, `kanban`, and `code_context` tools (single tool, op-dispatch — NOT a tool-per-verb). The tool is a thin wrapper: it parses the op + args, calls the engine `scope_review → run_fleet → verify_findings → synthesize`, and returns the structured `ReviewReport`. No orchestration logic lives in the tool.
+
+Ops (finalize names against the design doc, mirror the git tool's verb-noun convention):
+- `run review` — inputs: `{ range? , branch? , task_id? , dimensions? (subset), backend? ("session"|"local") }`; output: the `ReviewReport` (markdown + counts + the confirmed/refuted breakdown).
+- `list dimensions` — return the available dimensions and which rules feed each (so users can scope a review).
+- (optional, if cheap) `explain rule` — return a rule body by name.
+
+- Register via the existing `register_*_tools()` → `ToolRegistry` pattern used by the git tool; follow its op-dispatch module layout (`mod.rs` dispatch + per-op submodules) and its request/response serde structs.
+- Resolve the agent `ConnectionTo<Agent>` and the CWD from the MCP session/work-dir, NOT `std::env::current_dir()` (see the session-cwd-for-tools convention — a tool defaulting to CWD=/ walked the whole FS and hung the agent).
+- This tool does NOT write to kanban — the skill does that. The tool returns findings; the skill records them.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A single op-dispatched `review` tool is registered and discoverable over MCP, with `run review` and `list dimensions` ops.
+- [ ] `run review` runs scope→fleet→verify→synthesize and returns the `ReviewReport`; `backend` and `dimensions` inputs are honored.
+- [ ] Op-dispatch shape, registration, and request/response structs mirror the existing git tool; no tool-per-verb.
+- [ ] Agent connection + CWD resolved from the session/work-dir, never `current_dir()`.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Real-pipeline integration test (the reference pattern from `tests/integration/semantic_search_e2e.rs`): temp git repo with a planted duplicate + a planted dead function, drive `run review` through the registered tool with the mock/playback agent, assert the returned report flags both under the right dimensions and severities.
+- [ ] `list dimensions` returns the taxonomy.
+- [ ] Tool registration test (the op appears in the registry); `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools review` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the registered-tool integration test first (real registry, real engine, scripted agent), then wire the thin dispatch. Reuse the git tool's module structure as the template. This is a real-path test, not a mock-boundary unit test.
\ No newline at end of file

+## Review Findings (2026-06-05 19:42)
+
+Verdict: blocking. The tool's structure, op-dispatch, registration, validator-introspection ops, CWD/session resolution, and DI seam are all correct and match the git-tool pattern and ARCHITECTURE.md tiers (no dependency cycle: `swissarmyhammer-tools` and `swissarmyhammer-validators` depend on the lower-tier `claude-agent` + `agent-client-protocol`, never on the `swissarmyhammer-agent` facade, which is the crate that depends on `-tools`). Shipping the loader-read ops live with the three `review` ops returning an actionable error until factory `01KTCJ0T1X5QWFREA3JCADY19P` wires the backend is an acceptable MVP split. However, the driver itself — the headline deliverable of THIS task — has a production-only correctness bug that the scripted test cannot catch, and that the server-wiring follow-up would wire a real backend straight into.
+
+Both suites pass and clippy is clean: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators --lib review` = 67 passed (incl. `review::drive::tests::review_working_drives_the_pipeline_over_a_scripted_agent`); `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools review` = 7 passed; `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-tools -p swissarmyhammer-validators --all-targets` = 0 warnings (files touched to force a real recompile).
+
+### Blockers
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/drive.rs` (`run_review_over_agent`) — **Double notification delivery in production.** The driver forwards the SAME backend `SessionNotification` stream into the single pool `notifier` over TWO paths: (1) `forward_task` draining the passed-in `notification_rx`, and (2) the `Client.builder().on_receive_notification(...)` handler catching what the agent re-emits over the connection. For a real `swissarmyhammer_agent::AcpAgentHandle` these are the same broadcast channel: `wrap_claude_into_handle`/`wrap_llama_into_handle` call `.with_spawned(... forward_session_notifications(bridge_rx, cx))`, which bridges the backend's broadcast onto `cx.send_notification` — i.e. onto the very connection path (2) subscribes to — while `notification_rx` (a `resubscribe()` of that same channel) feeds path (1). So every streamed `AgentMessageChunk` reaches the pool's `spawn_notification_collector` twice and `collect_response_content` concatenates the agent's reply twice, corrupting the JSON the fleet/verify parser reads (doubled array / doubled fenced block → parse failure or duplicated findings). Why the tests miss it: the scripted-agent harness feeds an empty `notification_rx` (`let (_tx, rx) = broadcast::channel(8)`) and streams only over the live connection, so exactly one path is ever live — the dual-path production case is never exercised. Fix: forward over exactly ONE path. Either (a) subscribe the pool exclusively to the agent's `notification_rx` and drop the connection `on_receive_notification` forwarder, or (b) rely solely on the connection bridge and stop draining `notification_rx`. Then add a driver test with BOTH a live `notification_rx` AND connection-bridged delivery of the same notification, asserting the collected response is not doubled.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/drive.rs` tests / `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/tests.rs` — The pipeline tests only cover the single-path (connection-only) notification flow; the production dual-path flow (live `notification_rx` + connection bridge from a real `AcpAgentHandle`) is untested, which is precisely what hides the blocker above. Add coverage that mints a handle shaped like the real one (a non-empty `notification_rx` whose contents are ALSO bridged onto the connection) so the collector's de-duplication (or single-path forwarding) is asserted under the production shape.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/mod.rs` (`execute`) — The empty-`op` default is expressed twice: `unwrap_or("review working")` and then the `"review working" | ""` match arm. Harmless, but a single source of truth (default the string to `"review working"` and drop the `""` arm, or vice-versa) would avoid the two-places-to-keep-in-lockstep.
\ No newline at end of file
